text: Section BDF Agent Pipeline. Tags BDF Pipeline Live updated. 2026-0520 parent. BDF Soccerbot, section. BDF Agent Pipeline Book Compiler and Story Generator. This document covers the three Claude-powered scripts that form the creative and knowledge retention layer of the BDF Pipeline. Book Compiler, Pi, MCP Book Compiler, Pi and Story Generator Pi. Note, warning book compiler. Pi was moved to C. Backslash Dev Backslash shared Backslash Book Compiler Backslash on 2026-0520 as a shared plug-in serving all brain-os books. C Book Compiler shared. Note, MCP Book Compiler Pi and Story Generator. Pi remain in C. Backslash Dev Backslash Projects Backslash Soccer Content Generator Backslash and share the project Venv and Env. Section Book Compiler. Pi BDF Knowledge Book Compiler. Section Purpose. Book Compiler Pi maintains a living technical book documenting the entire BDF Soccer Content Pipeline. Its job is to take raw session notes or transcripts and merge them into structured, long-form chapter files that accumulate knowledge across sessions. The book currently has 21 chapters covering everything from pipeline architecture to export sequences and library routing. Section, inputs and outputs. The script watches C. Backslash Knowledge Backslash BDF Backslash BDF Book Backslash incoming Backslash for TXT and MD files. Each file placed in that folder is treated as a new session document to be processed. Processed files are moved to incoming Backslash-processed Backslash. Files with unknown tags are quarantined to incoming Backslash-Review Backslash. Non-BDF content is moved to incoming rejected. Chapter Files are written to C. Backslash Knowledge Backslash BDF Backslash BDF Book Backslash chapters Backslash using file names like CH-04 LansdbRag TXT. After each compile run the script stitches all chapters into a single BDF, master, book a TXT at the book root. TTS audio is generated for each updated chapter using converter, Pi and TTS local. Pi, with the resulting MP3s stored in C, Backslash Knowledge Backslash BDF Backslash BDF Book Backslash Audio Backslash. A combined session audio file is also produced. Audio files are optionally synced to a Google Drive folder called BDF Book Audio. Section, Processing Logic. When a session file arrives, the compiler first looks for explicit chapter tags in the format CH-04 LansdbRag followed by content and end. Tagged sections are rooted directly to the matching chapter. Files without tags are first evaluated for BDF relevance using a Claude call. Non-BDF documents are rejected. BDF relevant untagged files go through chapter auto detection. Claude reads the first 2,000 characters and returns a chapter key and a confidence score from 1 to 10. Files scoring 7 or below are sent to review for manual tagging rather than being silently misclassified. Compilation itself is a Claude Claude Sonnet 4.6 call that either writes a first draft chapter or merges new session material into an existing draft. The model is instructed to preserve all technical details, paths, and engineering decisions while eliminating pure repetition. Token usage and cost are appended to cost log, TXT at the book root after every run. Section, Known Architecture Note. The cost constants in book compiler, Pi are still labeled Opus input cost per million and Opus output cost per million, but the model was switched to Claude Sonnet 4.6 on 2026-040 following the Opus cost spike. The constants are stale labels. The actual model used is Sonnet. Section, MCP Book Compiler. Pi, MCP Knowledge Book Compiler. Section, Purpose. MCP Book Compiler. Pi is a structural sibling of book compiler. Pi but manages a separate knowledge book for the Resolve MCP Server project. It watches C, Backslash Knowledge Backslash, MCP Backslash, MCP Book Backslash, Incoming Backslash, and writes chapters to C. Backslash Knowledge Backslash, MCP Backslash, MCP Book Backslash, Chapters Backslash. Section, Chapters set. The MCP Book has a distinct set of chapters covering DaVinci Resolve specific concerns. The free tier Nuntype Inventory, Bridge Reload Discipline, Windows Encoding Patterns, and the two-process MCP Bridge Architecture. Server API, Pi Plus Bridge. It also carries a full copy of the BDF Book Chapters, making it a dual-purpose compiler that can accept session notes from either the Resolve MCP project or the Soccer Content Pipeline into a single process. Section, Shared Logic. The Internal Architecture of MCP Book Compiler. Pi is identical to Book Compiler. Pi. The same extract tag sections, detect chapter, compile chapter, is BDF-related, stitch masterbook, run TTS, and sync audio to drive functions appear in both files. The two scripts diverge only in their BookRoot path, their chapters dict, and the fallback value for BookRoot in the Environment lookup. This duplication is intentional. The two knowledge books are kept completely independent so a run on one never touches the other. Section, Story Generator. Pi, Soccer Animation Story Generator. Section, Purpose. Story Generator. Pi generates short three-scene soccer story scripts intended for animation production using Cartoon Animator 5. Players are treated as fictional characters with defined personalities, voice styles, rivalries, and friendships based on their real-world reputations. The output is a complete screenplay style document with visual descriptions, dialogue, a Twitter caption, and hashtags. Section, Cast. The script maintains a hard-coded cast of 10 players. Holland, M. Bap, YAML, Vinicius, Bellingham, Salah, Pedri, Gavi, and Dric and DeBrine. Each entry records the players club, country, personality archetype, voice style description, rivalries, and friendships. This cast is the editorial identity of the BDF Animation Channel. Section, Scenario Templates. 10 Scenario Templates are defined covering UCL finals, World Cup clashes, training ground arguments, club swaps, record-breaking moments, injury crises, press conferences, red card penalties, transfer deadline days, and shootouts. Templates contain format placeholders, Player One, Team One, etc. that are filled in at generation time from the cast data. Section, Generation. A Generate Story Call sends a prompt to Claude Sonnet 46, using a system prompt that defines the output format. Title, duration, players, competition, then three scenes each containing a visual description and character dialogue lines, ending with caption and hashtags. Each story costs approximately 0.015. Output files are saved to output stories as TXT files named Story Players Time Stamp, TXT. The script supports three run modes via CLI, Players Plus scenario for a targeted story, Batch N for N Random Stories, and Interactive Mode which presents a numbered menu. Section, connection to Pipeline. Stories produced by Story Generator. Pire standalone scripts intended for manual handoff to the CA5 Animation Pipeline. They are not automatically injected into the content queue. The generated captions and hashtags are formatted to BDF Twitter Standards, 280 char max, 5 tags, and can be copy-pasted into the queue if needed. Section, connected to. BDF Canvas, Tools Registry, Lance DB Vector Store.
